summary: Excellent early‑70s soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix, highlighted by an epic Lovelight jam and strong Easy Wind performances.
review: The recording delivers a remarkably clean soundboard source – most listeners rate the audio around 9/10 with only minor vocal/high‑hat balance quirks. Musically the show shines thanks to a legendary NFA > Lovelight sequence, a soaring Easy Wind and solid renditions of Good Lovin' and Dancin', while Phil Lesh’s bass work is repeatedly praised as phenomenal.

ai_rating.rationale: Average user rating is 4 ★ with a strong consensus praising both the performance (especially Lovelight and Easy Wind) and the sound quality; only isolated comments note minor mix issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with very good instrument separation; a few reviewers note slightly off vocal/high‑hat levels and occasional sloppiness typical of early‑70s recordings, but overall the sound is rated 9/10.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Early‑70s Family Dog set with a solid first half and a second‑half that builds into the marathon Lovelight jam; overall flow is considered strong despite occasional sloppiness.

band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly praised as phenomenal – thumping bass, unbelievable harmony and driving the band’s energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: No specific performance comments; one reviewer notes high‑hat output slightly out of balance in the mix.
